artifacts.content:
From: Eric Martinez <eric.martinez1244@gmail.com>
To: Laura Martinez <laura.martinez@email.com>
Date: 2026-03-03 21:18
Subject: Juggling Elena’s stuff, Dad, and block party ideas

Hey love,

House is finally quiet, so I’m trying to dump everything out of my head.

First, your mom: I still can’t believe July will be 18 months clean for Elena. When I think back to that rough winter before she got into treatment, it feels like a different lifetime. I keep picturing her lighting a candle at that little vigil we did with the MRHN recovery folks last July on the green in Westwood. Maybe we should do something smaller this year, just family and a couple people from her group, to mark 2 years.

On the less great side, I talked to my dad tonight. He’s still stressing about the Racine DUI fallout from 2023, and the way it hangs over every job interview. He said the support rally we did that August in Westwood helped him feel less like a pariah for a minute, but the day-to-day is still heavy. I wish I could do more than just listen and send him job links.

Meanwhile I’m supposed to be the “fun block party guy,” sketching out where the Maple Street Strings stage goes and how to keep the 1200–1299 W Maple stretch clear enough so people who don’t care about any of this can still get to their driveways. It’s weird switching from talking to Dad about court dates to arguing over where the 400 sq ft tent should sit.

Anyway, thanks for holding all this with me. Let’s talk this weekend about what Elena would actually want in July instead of me just planning things around her.

– E

metadata.subject: Juggling Elena’s stuff, Dad, and block party ideas

artifacts.content:
From: Eric Martinez <eric.martinez1244@gmail.com>
To: Marisol Martinez <marisol.martinez@email.com>
Date: 2026-02-10 22:04
Subject: All the “cause” events I keep dreaming up

Hey sis,

Couldn’t sleep, so I started scrolling old photos and realized how many weird little community events we’ve pulled off the last few years.

Remember when we were brainstorming names for Sofi’s pump fundraiser and landed on that mouthful about “Westwood Neighborhood Fundraiser for Sofia Martinez’s Type 1 Diabetes Pump & Supplies – $4,800 Goal by September 2024”? It sounded like a grant proposal, but people actually showed up, and we hit the goal before Labor Day. I still get choked up thinking about the jars of singles from neighbors we barely knew.

Then there was the co‑parenting picnic idea in 2024 when Laura and I were butting heads more. I almost called it the “Westwood Co‑Parenting & Custody Legal Aid Picnic – Supporting Caleb & Sofia Through 2024 Mediation.” We ended up working things out without dragging everyone into our business, but the idea stuck in my head – that other families might need that kind of space.

And now I’m talking with HR at MRHN about doing some kind of “staff burnout” concert. Part of me wants to be blunt and call it something like “MRHN Staff Burnout & Anxiety Awareness Concert – Sharing My 2023 Therapy Journey at Lakeside Behavioral Health,” because pretending I didn’t hit a wall in 2023 helps no one. But another part of me is like, do I really want my therapy story plastered on flyers in the cafeteria?

Anyway, this is me processing out loud. You’ve always been braver about putting your recovery stuff in the open. If you have energy this week, I’d love your read on how much honesty is too much.

Love you,
E

metadata.subject: All the “cause” events I keep dreaming up

artifacts.content:
Title: Big-picture community ideas dump
Date: 2026-01-28

Stuff swirling in my head that I don’t want to lose:

1) Recovery & mental health
- Talked with Father Miguel at St. Bridget’s about a June 15, 2026 “Community Recovery & Resource Fair” in the church parking lot. Goal: get folks like Elena and others from Westwood Behavioral Health Clinic (depression/anxiety groups) connected with resources without making it feel clinical.
- MRHN might send a licensed therapist to do up to 40 free 20‑minute depression screenings in a private tent. Need to make it crystal clear to Elena that it’s confidential and not some public confessional.

2) Second chances / legal stuff
- Been thinking about Luis Ramirez getting out of Racine Correctional in March 2026 after that 14‑month DUI/probation sentence. Idea: “Second Chance Community Support Fair” at Westwood Community Park on August 24, 2026 – job training booths, record‑expungement help, public defender’s table, plus a kids’ area so he can bring his 5‑year‑old.

3) Medical debt
- Dad’s still sitting on about $18,000 from his 2022 heart procedure at MRHN Racine Cardiac Center. Maybe a “Medical Debt & Charity Care Resource Fair” Sept 7, 2026 at Westwood High’s parking lot – MRHN billing advocates + Ruiz & Castillo Legal Clinic doing 30‑minute private consults (max 25 people).

4) Family support
- Marisol and Carlos are surprisingly open to sharing their 2023 Milwaukee Family Court custody mediation story and the ~$4,500 child support mess at some kind of May 10, 2026 “Co‑Parenting & Family Support Community Fair” at Westwood Civic Plaza.

Need to pace myself so this doesn’t become a second full‑time job.

From: Eric Martinez <eric.martinez1244@gmail.com>
To: Laura Martinez <laura.martinez@email.com>
Date: 2026-04-04 07:52
Subject: Saturday logistics (kids, Elena, and that bus stop)

Morning,

Before the day gets away from us, here’s what I’m thinking for the next block party planning Saturday.

We’re still closing the 1200–1299 W Maple stretch between N 12th and N 13th, basically right in front of our place (1244) and down to Mrs. Keller’s duplex at 1282 where Sofi’s bus comes at 7:25 a.m. I’ll make sure nobody starts dragging speakers or tent poles into that area until after the bus has come and gone so kids aren’t weaving through equipment.

I keep flashing back to July 3, 2023 when Officer Klein rolled up about the noise complaint while Elena’s oxygen delivery truck was blocking part of the street for 45 minutes. I don’t want a repeat of that vibe, especially now that she’s more stable and doesn’t need those deliveries. I’ll talk to the neighbors about keeping the early‑morning setup quiet and the driveway access clear.

For the kids: I told the committee I’d rotate into the kids’ zone from about 2:30–4:00 with your mom so I can keep an eye on Caleb. After Dr. Singh at West Allis Children’s Clinic flagged his impulsivity in June 2024 and the whole bolting‑from-the‑after‑school‑talent‑show incident in April, I really don’t want him parked next to the speakers all afternoon. We’ll keep him in the low‑key games area away from the loud stuff.

If you’re cool with this plan, I’ll update the volunteer schedule and text Mrs. Keller about the bus/closure timing.

– E

metadata.subject: Saturday logistics (kids, Elena, and that bus stop)

artifacts.content:
Title: Westwood Summer Neighborhood Block Party & Concert – full schedule + reminders
Date: 2026-08-08
Location: 1200–1299 W Maple St (between N 12th & N 13th), Westwood, WI 53214
Attendees: Eric, Laura, Sofia, Caleb, neighbors

All‑day notes:
- Street closure covers Sofi’s usual bus pickup spot in front of 1282 W Maple (Mrs. Keller’s duplex) – no school that day, but still keep setup clear by 7:25 a.m. out of habit.
- Remember last year’s MRHN phishing mess at the Racine clinic: use anonymized screenshots only for cyber‑safety talk, no actual names or addresses.

Schedule:
2:00–4:00 PM – Maple Street Strings (acoustic). Keep volume modest; older neighbors complained after the July 3, 2023 oxygen‑truck/noise‑complaint day.

2:30–4:00 PM – Kids’ area (mid‑block). I’ll rotate with Elena so we can keep an eye on Caleb and steer him toward chalk, beanbags, etc., away from loud music per Dr. Singh’s June 2024 impulsivity note.

3:00–3:20 PM – Cyber‑safety demo. Use redacted screenshots from Feb 2024 MRHN Racine phishing incident where Dad’s appointment reminder emails were spoofed. Emphasize: check sender address, don’t click links, call clinic directly.

4:00–5:00 PM – DJ set (me). Run playlists from MRHN‑approved laptop that IT cleared for off‑site use in March 2024 after the December 2023 VPN misconfiguration. No sketchy software, offline mode only.

5:00–7:30 PM – Westwood Groove Collective.

7:30–8:30 PM – DJ wind‑down set, keep it under city noise limits.

Post‑event: walk block with kids, pick up stray trash, check on older neighbors.

metadata.title: Westwood Summer Neighborhood Block Party & Concert – full schedule + reminders

artifacts.content:
From: Eric Martinez <eric.martinez1244@gmail.com>
To: Neighborhood Planning Thread <westwood-maple-block@lists.example.com>
Date: 2026-05-16 09:37
Subject: Entertainment rundown + quick safety notes

Hi all,

Here’s a more detailed rundown of the entertainment so we’re on the same page.

3:00–4:00 – Kids’ corner: sidewalk chalk, jump rope, beanbag toss, ring‑toss, etc. Elena and I will rotate in during the 2:30–4:00 window so I can keep an eye on Caleb; his pediatrician at West Allis Children’s Clinic flagged his impulsivity last June and suggested we avoid overstimulating setups after he bolted from his after‑school talent show in April. So we’ll keep that area a bit away from the main speakers.

3:30–3:50 – Cyber‑safety mini‑talk (me). I’ll bring anonymized screenshots from a phishing incident that hit our MRHN clinic in Racine in February 2024, when my dad’s patient reminder emails were spoofed. It’s a good real‑world example of how legit those scams can look and why people shouldn’t click links blindly.

4:30–5:00 – Neighborhood talent showcase. Sofia wants to perform a short dance she’s been working on in her trauma‑support group at Children’s Hospital of Wisconsin, the one they started after the May 12, 2023 crash on W. National Avenue when she and my mother‑in‑law were hit by a drunk driver. I’ve checked in with her therapist about it; they think it’ll be empowering as long as we keep it low‑pressure.

Between all that and the bands/DJ sets, we’ll post a printed schedule at both ends of the block and next to the food tables so folks know when the quieter moments are vs. louder music.

If anyone has concerns or ideas to make things calmer for sensitive kids or older neighbors, reply all and we’ll tweak.

– Eric

Title: Personal headspace check – late night
Date: 2026-01-05

Everyone’s asleep and I’m sitting in the office staring at the MRHN laptop like it’s a judgmental robot.

I keep replaying December 2023 in my head – that stupid VPN misconfiguration that briefly exposed non‑clinical staff scheduling data. Six‑month internal review, extra eyes on everything I touch. In March 2024 they finally signed off on a special exception so I could take this machine off‑site, but I still feel like security is breathing down my neck every time I open a browser tab. Even DJing the block party playlists from it feels risky, which is ridiculous.

The burnout signs from early 2023 are still there around the edges. Weekly 50‑minute sessions at Lakeside Behavioral Health helped a ton – I can hear my therapist’s voice when I start spiraling about tickets and outages – but I’m noticing the old patterns creeping back now that my manager is hinting about the lead engineer role. More responsibility probably means more 2 a.m. calls when a clinic’s Wi‑Fi dies.

On top of that, I’m carrying everyone else’s stuff: Elena’s recovery ups and downs, Dad’s DUI mess in Racine, Marisol’s relapse last October and her addiction ministry work at St. Francis, the kids’ needs (Sofi’s accident trauma, Caleb’s impulsivity). Then I turn around and try to be “community guy,” planning cyber‑safety demos and resource fairs like I’m not barely hanging on some days.

Need to remember: I’m allowed to say no. I’m allowed to be just a dad on Maple Street sometimes, not the guy with all the answers.

Notes to self:

- Event Involves Closing The Street No: false
- Type Of Event Church Festival: false
- Type Of Event Parade: false
- Type Of Event Carnival: false
- Alcohol Is Available Yes: true
- Alcohol Is Available No: false
- Electronic Amplified Sound Outdoors No: false
- Tent Or Structure 400 Feet Or Larger Present No: false
- Maximum Capacity Of The Premises: 250.0
